How to Use This

Five Things to Try

The map rewards exploration. Here's what to look for.

Start Here
Click Nvidia first

Nvidia is the most connected node on the map. Click it and watch every line light up — gold investment flows, teal partnerships, all 12 relationships visible at once. It shows you why Jensen Huang is arguably the most powerful person in AI.

Nvidia's connections include →
OpenAI · Anthropic · Meta · xAI · CoreWeave · Perplexity · HuggingFace · Cohere · Mistral · AMD · AWS · Azure
Follow the Money
Gold lines = billions

Every thick gold line is an investment. The thicker the line, the larger the deal. Microsoft → OpenAI ($13B) is the thickest on the map. Amazon → Anthropic ($4B) is next.

Filter Mode
Isolate any layer

Hit "AI Labs" to see only foundation model companies and their relationships. Hit "Enterprise" to understand how Salesforce, HubSpot, and SAP plug into the AI stack.

Hover
Highlight connections

Hover any node to dim everything else and see just that company's web.

Click Lines
Get the story

Every relationship line is clickable. See why the deal happened, what each side gained, and the dollar amount.

Alumni
Dotted = people

Gray dotted lines trace human departures. OpenAI → Anthropic is where 9 researchers left to build a safety-first competitor.

The 5 Line Types

Every Line Tells a Story

Investment
Equity or debt given. Thicker = more money.
Partnership
Strategic deal — API access, cloud agreement, co-development.
Alumni
Key people who left Company A to found Company B.
Owns
Full acquisition or majority ownership. Parent → Child.
Powers
AI provider relationship — this model runs inside that product.
Recent AI News

Where to Track AI

Six high-signal sources for model launches, funding rounds, policy moves, research shifts, and the companies shaping the map.

Live sources
Deep Tech

Best for slower, sharper context on labs, chips, policy, robotics, and the social impact behind each AI cycle.

Open coverage
Consumer

Fast, readable coverage of OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft, devices, apps, and the AI culture layer.

Open coverage
Startups

Useful for funding news, product launches, startup moves, founder activity, and the business side of AI platforms.

Open coverage
Enterprise

Strong signal on enterprise adoption, agent workflows, benchmarks, infrastructure, and how AI teams ship in companies.

Open coverage
Analysis

Longer reporting on AI power, security, regulation, data centers, labor, culture, and the people steering the industry.

Open coverage
Research

A weekly, practitioner-friendly read for AI research, model behavior, tooling, hardware, and technical trends that matter.

Open coverage
Questions

Frequently Asked

Core facts are verified to mid-2025. Major investments, ownership relationships, and model dependencies have been cross-checked. AI moves fast — some 2026 estimates (like valuations) are based on the most recent public information and may shift. When in doubt, company websites and SEC filings are the source of truth.
There are five line types: Gold solid lines = financial investment (money changing hands). Teal long-dashed lines = strategic partnerships. Gray dotted lines = alumni spinoffs (key people who left to found something new). Blue solid lines = ownership (company owns this product). Green short-dashed lines = AI powering a product. Click any line to get the full story behind it.
The map prioritizes the highest-impact nodes in each layer — companies that represent a distinct capability or relationship in the ecosystem. Some companies were excluded for being subsidiaries already represented by their parent, being too niche for a general audience, or because their AI story wasn't materially different from existing nodes. This is an evolving project and new nodes can always be added.
On mobile, use two fingers to pinch-zoom and one finger to pan. Tap any node to open its detail panel, which slides in from the right. Tap any connecting line for the relationship story. Use the filter buttons at the top to isolate specific layers of the ecosystem.
Nvidia makes the GPU chips that train and run ALL modern AI — without them, no modern AI exists at scale. Their H100 and H200 GPUs are essentially the oil wells of the AI economy. Nvidia has also strategically invested in or partnered with nearly every major AI company: OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, xAI, CoreWeave, Perplexity, HuggingFace, Cohere, and Mistral — making them the most connected node on the map.
The HTML file is fully self-contained — all logos, data, and code are embedded. You can save the page locally and it will work completely offline. For presentations, you can also take a screenshot of any state of the map (including with a specific company panel open).
